factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: Commercial rental pricing on average power units increased 9% in the third quarter of 2021 and 11% for the nine months ended September 30, 2021. ChoiceLease revenue increased 1% in the third quarter of 2021 and 2% for the nine months ended September 30, 2021 primarily due to higher prices, partially offset by lower revenue from a smaller fleet.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued) / Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company successfully raised prices, leading to revenue growth.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: FMS total revenue increased 11% in the third quarter of 2021 and 9% for the nine months ended September 30, 2021 due to higher operating revenue (a non-GAAP measure excluding fuel and ChoiceLease liability insurance revenues) and fuel revenue. FMS operating revenue increased 8% in both the third quarter and for the nine months ended September 30, 2021 primarily due to higher commercial rental revenue.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued) / Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, improving profitability.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating.

evidence.text: FMS total revenue increased 11% in the third quarter of 2021 and 9% for the nine months ended September 30, 2021 due to higher operating revenue and fuel revenue. SCS total revenue increased 17% in the third quarter of 2021 and 25% for the nine months ended September 30, 2021 as a result of higher operating revenue and subcontracted transportation. DTS total revenue increased 27% in the third quarter of 2021 and 14% for the nine months ended September 30, 2021 primarily due to higher subcontracted transportation and operating revenue growth.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: All end markets are growing rapidly, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: The company is investing in strategic initiatives to improve efficiency.

evidence.text: The company completed a review of the residual values and useful lives of revenue earning equipment, adjusting estimates to further reduce the probability of losses or need for accelerated depreciation during a potential cyclical downturn. The company is also investing in technology and strategic initiatives to improve efficiency.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is investing in efficiency improvements, which could enhance margins.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving through strategic initiatives.

evidence.text: The company completed a review of the residual values and useful lives of revenue earning equipment, adjusting estimates to further reduce the probability of losses or need for accelerated depreciation during a potential cyclical downturn. The company is also investing in technology and strategic initiatives to improve efficiency.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strategic initiatives are improving operational efficiency.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing.

evidence.text: Rental power fleet utilization increased to 83% from 71% in the third quarter of 2021 and increased to 79% from 64% in the nine months ended September 30, 2021. Commercial rental utilization — power fleet increased to 82.8% from 70.8% in the third quarter of 2021 and increased to 78.7% from 63.7% in the nine months ended September 30, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued) / Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Capacity utilization is significantly increasing, indicating better asset efficiency.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed efficiently.

evidence.text: NYE units increased as compared to September 30, 2020 reflecting increased rental capital spending to meet higher rental demand as well as higher lease sales activity. NLE units decreased 61% compared to September 30, 2020 due to a decrease in units held for sale, a lower number of vehicles being prepared for sale or redeployment and higher utilization of our rental fleet.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand shows mixed signals with some growth but supply chain issues.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly for fuel and vehicles, creating margin pressure. Higher fuel prices have led to increased revenue, but this has been partially offset by fewer gallons sold. Additionally, new regulations have contributed to higher prices for vehicles, diesel engines, and fuel.

evidence.text: Fuel services revenue increased 36% in the third quarter of 2021 and 25% in the nine months ended September 30, 2021 primarily reflecting higher fuel prices passed through to customers, partially offset by less gallons sold.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued) / Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

evidence.text: Higher prices for vehicles, diesel engines and fuel as a result of new regulations.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs pressure margins despite higher revenue.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to shortages and wage inflation, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: SCS EBT decreased 62% in the third quarter and 24% for the nine months ended September 30, 2021 primarily due to lower earnings in automotive, higher labor costs, and strategic investments, partially offset by earnings from new business. DTS EBT decreased 54% in third quarter of 2021 and 36% for the nine months ended September 30, 2021, primarily due to higher labor, increased insurance costs and strategic investments.

evidence.text: Difficulties in attracting and retaining drivers and technicians due to driver and technician shortages, which may result in higher costs to procure drivers and technicians and higher turnover rates affecting our customers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor shortages and wage inflation negatively impact margins.

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure and compressing due to higher labor and insurance costs, as well as supply chain disruptions. The evidence shows a consistent decline in EBT percentages across different segments, indicating significant margin contraction.

evidence.text: SCS EBT as a % of SCS total revenue decreased to 2.8% from 8.4% and SCS EBT as a % of SCS operating revenue decreased to 4.0% from 11.8%. DTS EBT as a % of DTS total revenue decreased to 3.0% from 8.3% and DTS EBT as a % of DTS operating revenue decreased to 4.2% from 10.6%

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Consistent margin compression across segments due to higher costs.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is facing challenges due to ongoing disruptions, extended lead times, and impacts on automotive production, though some segments are experiencing growth.

evidence.text: In 2021, supply chain disruptions impacted the production activity of our automotive customers through the third quarter of 2021, and is expected to continue through the fourth quarter of 2021.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals from management with both growth investments and caution.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include labor shortages, supply chain disruptions, and economic conditions, all of which are expected to negatively impact the company's financial performance and operations.

evidence.text: Labor shortages resulted in higher labor costs as well as higher subcontracted transportation costs, negatively impacting earnings. We expect the negative impacts from labor shortages to continue through the fourth quarter of 2021. In 2021, supply chain disruptions impacted the production activity of our automotive customers through the third quarter of 2021, and is expected to continue through the fourth quarter of 2021.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks like labor shortages and supply chain issues threaten performance.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, semiconductor shortages, and labor costs. These factors can have both positive and negative impacts on financial performance, with fuel price increases driving revenue growth but also contributing to higher costs. Labor shortages have led to elevated labor and transportation expenses, negatively affecting earnings. Additionally, semiconductor shortages and difficulties in attracting and retaining drivers and technicians further compound operational challenges.

evidence.text: Fuel services revenue increased 36% in the third quarter of 2021 and 25% in the nine months ended September 30, 2021 primarily reflecting higher fuel prices passed through to customers, partially offset by less gallons sold. Labor shortages resulted in higher labor costs as well as higher subcontracted transportation costs, negatively impacting earnings.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued) / Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

evidence.text: ◦ Sudden changes in fuel prices and fuel shortages ... ◦ Lower than expected sales volumes or customer retention levels ... ◦ Difficulties in attracting and retaining drivers and technicians due to driver and technician shortages ...
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities pose significant operational and financial challenges.
